evidence: BlackLabQueryExecutor.findCollocations and runDependencyQuery both iterate HitGroups, extract identity strings, build freqMap and lemmaPosMap, then call collocateQueryHelper.buildAndRankCollocates — structurally identical loops with only the extraction method differing (extractLemmaWithFallback vs extractCollocateLemma).

CollocateQueryHelper is a well-motivated extraction: it centralises headword frequency lookup, collocate grouping/search, and logDice ranking that were previously duplicated. The record type CollocateSearch cleanly returns paired results.

SketchHandlers.handleFullSketchForType and handleDepSketchForType are near-duplicates (~60% identical): both iterate over relations, call getFullPattern(lemma), call executeSurfacePattern, build a collocations list, and assemble a response map. The only differences are the DEP-specific deprel null-guard and the addition of a 'type':'dependency' field.

ExplorationHandlers has two explore handlers (single-seed, multi-seed) that share resolveRelationConfig, resolveExploreParams, buildBaseExploreResponse, and buildExploreResponseBody — this shared extraction is done well.
buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution in BlackLabQueryExecutor has a duplicated Javadoc comment block (two identical @see-style blocks for the method).
ExploreParams is a private record extracted only for use within ExplorationHandlers — appropriate scoping.
Deep nesting is generally absent; most methods use early returns. The query pipeline (executeCollocateSearch -> collectHits -> scoreHits) is properly separated into named stages.

strengths: CollocateQueryHelper cleanly extracts the shared collocate search/ranking pipeline from BlackLabQueryExecutor, following SRP.
ExplorationHandlers shares its common preamble logic (resolveRelationConfig, resolveExploreParams, buildBaseExploreResponse, buildExploreResponseBody) across single-seed and multi-seed handlers.
The three-phase pipeline in executeBcqlQuery (collect → score → rank) is named with clear method boundaries.
ExploreOptions and ExploreParams bundle related parameters, avoiding long argument lists.
design_coherence.issue_character: Residual structural duplication in SketchHandlers (surface vs dependency full-sketch loops) and in BlackLabQueryExecutor (findCollocations vs runDependencyQuery group-iteration), where each variation differs only in one extraction helper call.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase is well-above-average in design: it has already performed the key extractions (CollocateQueryHelper, shared explore helpers). The remaining duplication in SketchHandlers and the near-identical group-iteration loops in BlackLabQueryExecutor are real design debt — each represents an opportunity to unify via a strategy-style parameterisation. These patterns are confirmed, not false positives. Score of 71 reflects solid architectural intent with a handful of notable execution gaps.

issues.summary: handleFullSketchForType and handleDepSketchForType are near-duplicate loops differing only in DEP-specific fields
evidence: Both methods iterate grammarConfig.getRelations(), check relationType, call rel.getFullPattern(lemma), call executeSurfacePattern(..., 0.0, 20), build a collocations list via formatWordSketchResult, and assemble a byRelation/response map.
handleDepSketchForType adds a null-guard on rel.getDeprel() and a 'type':'dependency' field in the response — the only behavioural differences.
buildRelationResponse is already extracted as a private static but is only called from the DEP path; the SURFACE path inlines equivalent logic.
issues.suggestion: Merge into a single handleFullSketchForRelationType(HttpExchange, String lemma, RelationType) method, passing a boolean or enum to control the DEP-specific additions (deprel null-guard, 'type' field). Use buildRelationResponse for both paths.

issues.summary: findCollocations and runDependencyQuery repeat the same HitGroups-iteration structure with a single differing helper call
evidence: findCollocations lines 62-78: iterates groups, calls BlackLabSnippetParser.extractLemmaWithFallback, merges into freqMap/lemmaPosMap, then calls buildAndRankCollocates.
runDependencyQuery lines 148-163: iterates groups, calls BlackLabSnippetParser.extractCollocateLemma, merges into freqMap/lemmaPosMap, then calls buildAndRankCollocates.
Both methods share the same merge pattern (freqMap.merge(key, group.size(), Long::sum)) and the same conditional pos extraction.
issues.suggestion: Extract a private buildFreqMaps(HitGroups, Function<String,String> lemmaExtractor) that returns a pair of freqMap/lemmaPosMap, and call it from both findCollocations and runDependencyQuery with the appropriate lemma-extractor lambda.

issues.summary: buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution has two identical Javadoc comment blocks consecutive
evidence: Two consecutive /** ... */ Javadoc blocks appear immediately before the private static buildBcqlWithLemmaSubstitution method; both describe the same canonical vs legacy format semantics.
issues.suggestion: Remove the first (now-stale) Javadoc block; retain only the second, updated version.

root_causes: Incremental feature addition (DEP sketch added after SURFACE sketch) without unifying the handler template.
Two similar collocation workflows (general CQL vs dependency BCQL) evolved in parallel before CollocateQueryHelper was extracted, leaving residual structural similarity in the executor.
